text.title: Multi-Touch Attribution Modeling Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Multi-Touch Attribution Modeling MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Spec MUST cover first-touch + linear + position-based + time-decay. Single-model attribution is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Different channels favor different models; comparing four variants reveals channel mis-attribution.

rule.statement: Warehouse (BigQuery / Snowflake) MUST be the reconciled source of truth. Platform self-reports are inputs, not outputs.
rule.rationale: Platform self-reports double-count and use different windows; only warehouse can dedup.

rule.statement: Variance > 15% between platform-reported / GA4 / warehouse MUST trigger investigation before budget decisions.
rule.rationale: Above 15% indicates pixel misfire, dedup failure, or definition drift; acting on bad data destroys budget.

rule.statement: Model MUST track 'direct/none' as a named channel and investigate sources (community / podcast / DM / referral).
rule.rationale: 30-50% of B2B pipeline is dark-social; ignoring it under-credits the channels that actually fed it.

rule.statement: Reconciliation MUST run monthly with named owner; quarterly is too slow, weekly is over-fit to noise.
rule.rationale: Monthly catches drift before quarter-end budget meetings; weekly causes flip-flop reallocation.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
